description: Richly ripe wine, dominated by sweet yellow fruit, although with a fine line of acidity that brings a greener element. Wood spice comes through on the finish of this full-bodied wine.
title: Quinta de Ventozelo 2010 Branco White (Douro)

description: This Pinot saw one-quarter new oak, which contributed an undertone of burnt toast. The spicy berry fruit is accented by a cola note, and the wine has a friendly, mouth-filling roundness. This is perfect for near term drinking.
title: J. Scott Cellars 2010 Pinot Noir (Oregon)

description: This pretty expression of Primitivo opens with ripe fruit aromas of prune and blackberry, plus notes of sweet spice and moist pipe tobacco. There's a soft, chewy feeling on the palate, followed by bold fruit flavors.
title: Felline 2010  Primitivo di Manduria

description: This is a very pretty wine, though not nearly as substantial as the 2009. Fruit flavors of strawberry and light cherry are set over tart acids. The palate softens up a bit and runs through a quick, clean fade.
title: Raptor Ridge 2010 Adalfo's Block Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: A sound, bistro-style wine that's dry and full-bodied. Made from Syrah and Grenache, it shows tobacco, cherry and spice flavors, with brisk acidity.
title: Morgan 2010 Cotes du Crow's Red (Monterey)

description: Strong notes of lemongrass and pineapple jam mark this Sauvignon Blanc. It has a nice cut of acidity. While it's not particularly subtle, this is ready to drink now as a cocktail.
title: Ojai 2010 McGinley Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Acidity dominates this Chardonnay despite rich pineapple, orange and apricot flavors. It's rather oaky, with a flavor of buttered toast. Made in the popular style, it's for drinking now.
title: Graton Ridge 2010 Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Strong classy oak aromas lead blackberry and baking-spice scents on a potent but textbook RDD nose. This old-vines wine from a benchmark year is tight-fisted and linear in feel, with lemony acidity. Fully oaked flavors of blackberry and cassis sit comfortably on a bed of herb and spice notes, while this is oaky, spicy, steady and integrated on the finish. Drink through 2027.
title: Alidis 2010 VS  (Ribera del Duero)

description: Very Douro in its mineral texture and dry core, it also has a surround of ripe black fruits. It's concentrated and has some aging potential, although it can be drunk now.
title: Quinta de Porrais 2010 Porrais Red (Douro)

description: This dark-tinted Cab shows aromas of blackberry, spice and olive along with some grit and herbal character. The palate is jammy but propped up by juicy acidity. Herbal-leaning flavors of blackberry, black plum and cocoa finish fresh and snappy. Drink through 2017.
title: Viña Casas Patronales 2010 Ñeque Cabernet Sauvignon (Maule Valley)

description: A blend of Viognier and Roussanne, this wine is way too sweet. Tastes like a sugared orange and pineapple fruit juice drink. Smoky, caramelized oak makes it even sweeter and cloying.
title: Curtis 2010 Heritage Blanc White (Santa Barbara County)

description: Givry, which is located in the Côte Chalonnaise, produces wines that have warmth and a firm edge. This wine fits the style with its juicy red fruits, ripe tannins and solid, chunky finish. This is not for aging, so drink now.
title: Jaffelin 2010 Les Grandes Vignes Premier Cru  (Givry)

description: This blend of Merlot (61%), Cabernet Sauvignon (27%), Syrah (8%) and Petit Verdot brings notes of anise, black currant, herbs, mint and raspberries. The palate brings good depth of flavors and richness while showing both elegance and precision.
title: Northstar 2010 Stella Maris Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Heavily toasted aromas promise strong wood flavors. The fruit, rich with pear flavors, balances that wood, although the wine does need a few months more integration.
title: Campolargo 2010 Branco Arinto (Bairrada)

description: 92-94 Full of toasty flavors, this is a structured, wood and ripe fruit dominated wine. It has weight, while also feeling fresh. Great final spice and pear flavors.
title: Château Malartic-Lagravière 2010 Barrel sample  (Pessac-Léognan)

description: 91-93 A fresh, while structured wine, full of sweet acidity, complex tannins and a rounded, ripe character. The fruit is tough, while also generous.
title: Château Grand-Puy Ducasse 2010 Barrel sample  (Pauillac)

description: 91-93 This feels very juicy, the fruit almost over-ripe. There is a generous new world feel to it, very opulent, super-rich. Open black plum and damson fruits push through the dark, perfumed tannins.
title: Château Pavie 2010 Barrel sample  (Saint-Émilion)

description: A touch of oak adds smoky vanilla and toast to this Chardonnay, but what stars is the fruit. Pineapples, limes, green apples and kiwis blast through to a spicy finish, brightened by crisp acidity.
title: Kenwood 2010 Chardonnay (Sonoma County)

description: Tight, mineral wine, with apple and spice flavors. There is some weight as well as clean green fruits. There is an attractive fresh feel here, very drinkable now, best with food. Screwcap.
title: Ebner-Ebenauer 2010 Bürsting Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

description: Dark and richly colored, this juicy, mouthwatering, generously oaked wine has a high quotient of blackberry and raspberry. It is as rich on the palate as it is in color, getting better in the glass.
title: Wattle Creek 2010 Vintage Select Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)

description: This is a soft, seemingly sweet, simple wine. Although it's luscious in raspberry, cherry, orange zest and cinnamon spice notes, it's pretty one-dimensional.
title: Standing Sun 2010 Grenache (Santa Barbara County)

description: An open-book Cabernet medium in tannin structure and expressive in plum, blackberry and tobacco. Juicy with a rustic edge.
title: Loken Cellars 2010 Penn Valley Vineyards Cabernet Sauvignon (Sierra Foothills)

description: Concentrated and generous in sweet red fruit, this is a gangbuster Oakville Merlot for the price. Balanced in fruit, herb and dry tannin, it's a lovable quaff from a well-known vineyard.
title: Gibbs 2010 Money Road Merlot (Oakville)

description: Pure Merlot from the Red Willow vineyard, this does not skimp on the scents and flavors of green herb. It's true to the vineyard's style and will require further bottle age to flesh out a bit and show its best strengths.
title: Barrage Cellars 2010 A.K.A. Merlot (Yakima Valley)

description: From one of the best producers in the little-traveled region of Molise on Italy's Adriatic flank, this wine shows the far reaches of the Aglianico grape in southern Italy. This expression shows bright fruit tones of blackberry and cherry, backed by velvety tannins.
title: Di Majo Norante 2010 Contado Riserva Aglianico (Molise)

description: Preserved red-cherry and strawberry notes penetrate throughout this ripe, full-bodied Pinot Noir. Bright acidity and hints of brambly wood, baking spice and florals on the palate add complexity and balance.
title: Damiani 2010 Sunrise Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Finger Lakes)

description: This is a strongly flavored Chardonnay. It packs a punch in ripe tropical citrus fruit, pear and vanilla oak flavors that are honey sweet on the finish. The acidity provides balance, but seems awkwardly applied.
title: VJB 2010 Gabriella Ranch Chardonnay (Sonoma Valley)

description: Although this wine sees contact with stainless steel only (no oak), there is a carbonized, toasted quality to the fruit regardless. The mouthfeel is characterized by ripe cherry, dried lavender and spicy church incense.
title: Buglioni 2010 Il Valpolicella  (Valpolicella Classico)

description: Green bean and lettuce aromas run the tight, green nose straight into a lip-smacking, acidic palate with tart apple and green citrus flavors, i.e. lime. Crisp and acidic on the finish, with a minerally feel.
title: Viña Tabalí 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (Limarí Valley)

description: Direct and appealing with notes of dried herbs, cherry, and toast. It's broad in feel with good depth and structure with dry tannins the only thing holding it back.
title: Northwest Collection 2010 Limited Release Merlot (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: There is as much perfume as sweetness in this rich, opulent wine. It has an oriental-spice character along with rosewater and honey notes. The wine needs to be aged so drink from 2016.
title: Kracher 2010 Zwischen den Seen Nummer 2 Trockenbeerenauslese Muskat Ottonel (Burgenland)

description: Dense and dark, this has earthy, crusty berry aromas and a sprinkle of herb on the nose. It hits with aggression but settles down nicely to offer raspberry, plum and savory herb flavors. Lively acidity allows for a long finish.
title: Marqués de Montañana 2010 Selección Especial Garnacha (Calatayud)

description: This is a tight, currently closed wine, but it has the potential to exude intense fruit and balancing acidity. It has weight and a taut, minerally texture, and the finish is fresh and lively. It will age over the medium term.
title: Olivier Leflaive 2010 Grande Montagne Premier Cru  (Chassagne-Montrachet)

description: Subtly scented with orange rind and spice, this dry Michigan Riesling has pleasant apple and orange zest flavors accented by brisk black tea notes that leave a pronounced astringency on the palate. An easy-drinking choice from an up-and-coming wine region.
title: Chateau Grand Traverse 2010 Dry Riesling (Old Mission Peninsula)

description: Tough in tannins and bone dry, this Merlot's raisin and blackberry fruit simply isn't strong enough to stand up to the astringency.
title: Parkers Estate 2010 North Peyton Block Merlot (Sonoma County)

description: A soft, open wine with only light tannins. The fruit is just as light, with strawberry and some spice. For drinking now.
title: Finisterra 2010 Tinto Red (Alentejano)

description: Co-fermented with 5% Viognier, this Syrah is direct and appealing with notes of boysenberry jam, smoked meat and orange peel. The palate is soft and sumptuous in feel with tart fruit flavors.
title: Cinder 2010 Syrah

description: The fruit of this Pinot Noir is fantastically ripe, a testament to the California sun and the long growing season of the Santa Lucia Highlands. The wine is enormous in raspberries and cherries that are jammy, yet finish dry. The acidity also is notable, cutting the richness with citrusy tartness that may limit ageability. Drink now–2016.
title: Furthermore 2010 Sierra Mar Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Here's a gorgeous Chianti Classico that shows great intensity, breeding and class. It opens with quintessential varietal aromas of leather, blue flower, berry and spice. The palate delivers juicy black cherry, white pepper, cinnamon and mint alongside bracing but refined tannins and fresh acidity. It already has compelling depth but is still young and needs time to develop even more complexity. Drink 2016–2030.
title: Monteraponi 2010 Baron' Ugo Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: From a superior block at the Red Willow vineyard, this pure Syrah was aged in 100% new French oak. It's concentrated and tangy, with wild berry fruit streaked with oolong tea, licorice, dark chocolate and coffee liqueur.
title: Barrage Cellars 2010 Red Willow Chapel Block Syrah (Yakima Valley)

description: This wine exhibits pristine Syrah character from the first whiffs of cracked peppercorns, herbs and berries. It's only medium to full bodied, yet finishes long, picking up hints of espresso as it fades.
title: Mills Reef 2010 Elspeth Gimblett Gravels Syrah (Hawke's Bay)

description: The nose is loaded with mineral, pencil lead and blackberry notes. Generously juicy on the palate, this exudes youthful fruit backed by terroir-influenced notes. The tannins are structured and lightly sinewy yet soft all at once. The acidity is fresh, carrying the well-concentrated flavors of this chunky wine into a medium finish. This is a decidedly Old World in style, with evident fruit purity and provenance of place. Drink now and for three more years.
title: Château Saint Baulery 2010 Red (Saint-Chinian)

description: A blend of 80% Sangiovese, 10% Canaiolo and 10% Cabernet Sauvignon, this starts with aromas of red berry, blue flower and spice. The juicy palate serves up black cherry and cinnamon-spice along with notes of espresso. It's soft and one-dimensional but delicious none the less.
title: Banfi 2010 Riserva  (Chianti Classico)

description: The tech sheet for this wine nicely mentions how the vineyard is surrounded by both 100-year-old walnut trees and, what do you know?, marijuana. Does either influence the wine? It does show a muskiness but really its strengths are steely drops of extreme lemon, most decidedly on the long, exuberant finish.
title: Rock Wall 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (Lake County)

description: Balsam wood, coconut, tobacco and black-fruit aromas feed into a lush, easygoing palate with smooth tannins and lots of weight. This is ripe in raisin and prune flavors along with savory berry, cumin and baking spice notes. A chunky finish delivers chocolate and tobacco flavors but not much bite or structure. Drink through 2019.
title: Matarromera 2010 Reserva  (Ribera del Duero)

description: This structured yet polished wine, made from a blend of 50% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Aglianico and 20% Merlot, opens with intense aromas of tilled earth, mature black fruit, leather, graphite and spice. The Juicy palate offers fleshy blackberry, black currant, black pepper and mineral alongside silky smooth tannins. Hold for more complexity. Drink 2015–2025.
title: Montevetrano 2010 Montevetrano Red (Colli di Salerno)

description: From the winery that first popularized Petite Sirah in California, this vintage has vivid black-pepper aromas and a deep, inky color. Rich and concentrated flavors suggest pomegranate juice, blackberries and clove. Lavish and layered tannins give the texture an almost thick viscosity that coats the mouth for a lasting impression.
title: Concannon 2010 Reserve Petite Sirah (Livermore Valley)

description: If you're curious about California Grenache Blanc, check out this one. It shows beautifully varietal flavors of oranges, pineapples and honey, with vital Santa Ynez acidity. So rich, it's hard to believe it never saw any oak.
title: Coghlan 2010 Tourmaline Grenache Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: What a delicious Zin this is. Sourced from the warm climates of Lodi and Amador County, it's ripe and fruity, offering waves of wild raspberries and cherries dusted with brown sugar, cocoa and pie spice. Great value in a lusty, versatile Zin.
title: Bogle 2010 Old Vine Zinfandel (California)

description: The soft but exotic nose of tropical fruit and citrus leads into an elegant array of mouthwatering citrus and tropical fruit on this wine. Rich but lively on the finish, it's a classy sip for a warm-weather patio party.
title: Lyrarakis 2010 Plyto (Crete)

description: Fairly neutral on the nose, but clean and easy to get into. The palate offers good acidity and vitality for Maule SB, with tropical fruit leading the flavor profile followed by pithy green notes. Overall this is a good value white.
title: Viña Requingua 2010 Puerto Viejo Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Maule Valley)

description: Fresh and food friendly, this has light aromas of citrus, peach and caramel. The wine is immediate and direct thanks to its natural freshness.
title: Villa Rubini 2010 Pinot Grigio (Colli Orientali del Friuli)

description: The bilingual region of Alto Adige refers to this variety as Pinot Bianco in Italian or Weissburgunder in German. This expression is fresh and crisp, with dry mineral shadings between tones of citrus and peach.
title: Niedermayr Josef 2010 Pinot Bianco (Alto Adige)

description: The entry level wine from Eugénie, this is a smooth, ripe wine, juicy and structured enough to give weight to the texture. The black currant fruits are already blossoming through the tannins. The wine should be ready to drink in 2014.
title: Château Eugénie 2010 Tradition Malbec-Merlot (Cahors)

description: Pithy and citrusy on the nose, but ultimately it's rather neutral. The palate is citric and lean, with orange, lemon and sour apple flavors. Zesty to the point of tartness, with a citrusy, juicy finish. A pointed SB with zing.
title: Emiliana 2010 Natura Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: Oak-driven aromas recall toast, espresso and coconut. The palate offers steeped plum, dried cherry, orange zest, grilled sage and flint, with vigorous tannins. A licorice note signals the close.
title: Pietroso 2010  Brunello di Montalcino

description: Aromas of ripe plum, sunbaked earth and toast lead the way on this structured red. The palate offers dried black cherry, ground pepper and toasted oak while dusty tannins give it a firm, slightly astringent finish.
title: Baglio di Pianetto 2010 Cembali Nero d'Avola (Sicilia)

description: A tarry, soft, straight-shooting red wine with a subtle herbal component, touch of anise and long finish. There is excitement building in the Sierra Foothills about the potential for Cab Franc and this is a fine place to start exploring. Pair with a meat—chicken, lamb or pork—richly adorned in herbs to bring out the wine's herbal notes more fully.
title: Metaphora 2010 Grace, Hope & Joy Cabernet Franc (Sierra Foothills)

description: Lots of zippy acidity in this fresh, dry, clean Sauvignon Blanc. It's unoaked, which allows the Meyer lemon, orange and guava fruit to star. There's a sweet vanilla taste that must come from the grapes. A beautiful, complex white wine to drink now.
title: Brander 2010 Au Naturel Sauvignon Blanc (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: This is a lean Dominus, showing the difficulties of the vintage. It's dry and tannic, with black currant, cherry and cassis flavors that are wrapped in strong, hard tannins. It's a good wine, but lacks the r ich complexity you expect from this property.
title: Dominus 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: A lightly perfumed wine, this is soft with warm strawberry flavors. The acidity and texture play a supporting role in this ripe, light-bodied wine.
title: Manuel Olivier 2010  Bourgogne

description: A successful expression of Sauvignon Blanc, this has good structure, density and staying power. It delivers stone fruit, citrus, pear, exotic fruit flavors, with touches of spice and green herb on the crisp close.
title: Lis Neris 2010 Picol Sauvignon Blanc (Friuli Isonzo)

description: A maderized tint and baked, raisiny flavors make for a rich but flat bouquet. Following suit, this feels heavy, with fig-like richness and choppy tannins. Flavors of raisin, vanilla, coconut and stewed berry fruits end chunky, with spice, vanilla and saucy notes.
title: Hesvera 2010 Cosecha Limitada  (Ribera del Duero)

description: Aromas of stewed prune and fig follow through to the evolved palate along with raisin, licorice and a green note of powdered sage. Austere, astringent tannins leave a raspy finish. You'll also notice the heat of evident alcohol.
title: Val di Suga 2010 Vigna Spuntali  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: This is 100% Syrah from the Seven Hills vineyard, a firm and tannic effort with dark, rich flavors. Cassis, black pepper, coffee grounds and gravelly mineral flavors combine in a muscular wine with big bones and a finishing lick of citrus.
title: Tyrus Evan 2010 Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (OR))

description: The main issue with this blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah is a streak of vegetal unripeness that accompanies the cherries and blackberries. The second problem is excessive softness. Drink up.
title: Heartfelt 2010 Inspiration Red (Napa Valley)

description: Despite ample alcohol levels and soft tannins, this wine comes across as tart and crisp on the palate, its cola, cherry and vanilla flavors ending on a lemony note.
title: Plantagenet 2010 Omrah Pinot Noir (Great Southern)

description: Sprightly and crisp, with just 7% alcohol, this dainty Riesling is juicy and brisk, with loads of sweet-tart tangerine flavor. Layers of bitter citrus rind and lanolin, along with a steely midpalate, add depth, extending to a long, lemony finish.
title: Dr. Loosen 2010 Erdener Prälat Auslese Gold Capsule Riesling (Mosel)

description: So soft, the wine is gently fruity, a hint of its chalk soil through a mineral edge. Fresh and tasting of lime and apple fruits, it is ready to drink now.
title: Domaine L. Chatelain 2010  Chablis

description: Grassy red berry and plum aromas lead to a lean, somewhat high-acid palate with herbal, oaky flavors of raspberry and plum that don't last that long. Fresh and healthy feeling on the finish, but tasting more of oak and spice than fruit.
title: Maipe 2010 Andean Culture Malbec (Mendoza)

description: This Cabernet isn't particularly Rutherford-esque due to excessive softness and fruitiness. It could come from almost anywhere, with its raspberry and cherry jam, milk chocolate and toast flavors.
title: Daglia Canyon 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Rutherford)

description: The best of CVNE's impressive lineup of 2010 high-end Riojas is this pure, staunch, ripe stud that's blasting with blackberry, woodspice, leather and cedar aromas. Lush, layered and deep as an ocean trench, this ultraripe, expressive wine pumps out blackberry, cassis and chocolate flavors prior to a lush, mile-long finish. Amazingly pure and delicious; drink through 2025.
title: CVNE 2010 Real de Asúa  (Rioja)

description: Aromas of cassis, dried flower and dill are accented by notes of turmeric and desiccated black fruit. Medium to full bodied, this blend of 50% Merlot and 50% Cabernet Sauvignon is juicy and supple, with some chalky tannins that add tension. Red fruit and espresso notes creep onto the midpalate, lending extra nuance.
title: Tarara 2010 Winemaker's Select Bin #9 Merlot-Cabernet Sauvignon (Virginia)

description: This is a light and fruity wine that has some attractive red berry flavors and lively acidity. There is a tannic background that gives the a sense of structure, although the dry core lacks some fruit tones.
title: Quinta Santa Eufemia 2010 Late Bottled Vintage  (Port)

description: Sigalas sets the bar high for Assyrtiko as usual. This 2010 is a balance of rounded citrus, crisp sea salt and sparkling minerality. Delicious with grilled fish, fruit or alone on a hot day, this is an elegant gem of a white that represents some of the best of Greek wine offered today.
title: Domaine Sigalas 2010 Assyrtico (Santorini)

description: Kudos to Ste. Michelle for accurately labeling this wine, and also for the Riesling sweetness scale on the back of all their Rieslings. Firm Yakima Valley fruit is the backbone here, tart and spicy, with bright flavors of pear and apple. A juicy, palate-cleansing style.
title: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2010 Dry Riesling (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Rounded and soft, this is an attractive, warm wine. Pears and green plums pair with the acidity. A final burst of citrus marks the finish. Ready to drink as a friendly apéritif.
title: Domaines Schlumberger 2010 Les Princes Abbés Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Dense and sweet up front, the nose is balanced and deep, with blackberry and graham cracker aromas. The palate feels grippy and concentrated, bearing blackberry, dark plum and black pepper flavors, with a lifted flower note. This finishes mild and spicy.
title: Familia Marguery 2010 Casa Marguery Malbec-Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendoza)

description: Orange marmalade and orgeat syrup hint at the sweeter side of this wine, but there is also a sharp and interesting line of chopped basil and cut scallions on the nose. The palate is indeed sweet, with honeysuckle and apple-lemon custard flavors, but shows an herbal compost and quinine element as well.
title: EOS 2010 Muscat Canelli (Paso Robles)

description: This high-production Pinot shows good varietal flavors of red cherry candy, cola and spices. It feels common, with a simple finish.
title: A By Acacia 2010 Pinot Noir (California)

description: This is soft and smooth, with acidity and a creamy character. The flavors are reminiscent of ripe pear and spice. Very ready to drink.
title: Bott Frères 2010 Pinot Blanc (Alsace)

description: Made in a style to appeal to many Chardonnay drinkers, this one seems to have sweetness, with flavors of custard, vanilla, butter cream and orange jam.
title: Churchill Cellars 2010 Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: Tilled soil, mature berry, menthol, leather and a suggestion of game unfold on this delicious, full-bodied red. The structured, polished palate doles out juicy Morello cherry, raspberry jam, cinnamon, chopped herb and licorice while firm, refined tannins lend finesse and a silky texture. Drink 2018–2028.
title: Terralsole 2010 Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: This is a perfumed wine, and its new wood and violet aromas create equally pleasant flavors. The surface-level brilliance is followed by rich, dark tannins and a blackberry note.
title: Château La Mothe du Barry 2010 Cuvee le Barry  (Bordeaux Supérieur)

description: This is a perfumed wine with fine acidity. The tannins are soft, accompanied by black cherry and ripe berry notes, making this a sweet, rich style. The Quinta Senhora de Ribeira is on the north bank of the Douro, facing Quinta do Vesúvio on the south bank.
title: Dow's 2010 Quinta Senhora da Ribeira Vintage  (Port)

description: Hedonistic berry, raisin and fig flavors are complete. While this is tight and tough on the palate, it's solid as a boulder in terms of structure and balance. A ripe mix of black-fruit and mocha flavors finishes with all the power one could ask for. Drink this Bordeaux blend through 2027.
title: Antigal 2010 Aduentus Classic Red (Mendoza)

description: This will divide the Zinfandel community. Some will find its earthiness and herbaceousness an attractive complication to the briary berry and spice notes. Others will think it's not quite ripe enough and lacking richness. The score reflects the wine's complexity and adaptability to a wide range of foods.
title: Ridge 2010 Ponzo Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

description: This has grassy tones and tropical fruit flavors that'll pair with oysters and other shellfish just fine.
title: Souverain 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (North Coast)

description: From the Messina area in northern Sicily, this is a 50-50 blend of Nero d'Avola and Nerello Mascalese that bursts with bright berry freshness. The finish is clean, bright and tight.
title: Cambria 2010 Bazia Rosso Red (Sicilia)

description: The price on this wine is laughable, but the quality is excellent. Earthy aromas of lead pencil, baked plum, cassis and black cherry include chocolate and minerality. The palate is rich, jammy and a touch stewy, with herbal blackberry, cassis, chocoalte and coffee flavors. Even more chocolate and licorice rise up on the long finish. Put this away until 2015, then drink through 2024.
title: Errazuriz 2010 Viñedo Chadwick Red (Maipo Valley)

description: Concentrated and elusively complex in cherry, red currant and sandalwood flavors, with a hint of red tomato and bloody red meat that testifies to the cool vintage. It's a big, dense wine, but also quite tannic, and a little high in alcohol, giving it a full body. A bit ungainly now, but impressive nonetheless for sheer power. Give it six years in the cellar.
title: Stemmler 2010 Nugent Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This tastes sweet and hot, which gives the flavors of blackberry and raisin a Port-like character.
title: Tin Barn 2010 Gilsson Vineyard Zinfandel (Russian River Valley)

description: Acidity dominates this wine, giving it a tartness—like a squeeze of lime juice. Past that is an enormously rich, dry, vibrant wine, explosive in raspberries, cherries and ripe, sweet persimmons. New French oak, with its caramel-like toastiness, is perfectly in keeping with the wine's volume. It's a bit disjointed now, but give it 3–4 years in the cellar, and it should come around.
title: Dutton-Goldfield 2010 Dutton Ranch Freestone Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: Tense, dry wine that brings out all the minerality of the Käferberg Vineyard. This is an impressive, ageworthy wine, dominated at this stage by citrus and green apple flavors. Likely to age over many years with its structure intact.
title: Bründlmayer 2010 Käferberg Erste Lage Reserve Grüner Veltliner (Kamptal)

description: This shows the dry tannins and heady alcohol that so often mark a young Petite Sirah. It's full bodied and lusty, with ripe blackberry jam, currant and oak flavors. It's a little on the rustic side, but fine with barbecue.
title: Rosenblum 2010 Pickett Road Reserve Petite Sirah (Napa Valley)

description: This Pinotage-dominant blend is a bit closed to begin, but opens up after some time in the glass to reveal enticing notes of dark berries, plums and whole vanilla beans. Medium tannins and jammy, crushed-berry fruit flavors flesh out the palate, ending on a textured plum-skin flavor.
title: Kanonkop 2010 Kadette Red (Stellenbosch)

description: This offers much of the intensity of Amarone with some of the easy-drinking appeal of Valpolicella. Twists of ripe cherry and dried ginger lend it extra personality.
title: Castellani Michele & Figli 2010 San Michele Ca' del Pipa  (Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso)

description: Luscious tropical fruit and jasmine notes are framed by hints of fresh green herb and grass. A spray of lemony acidity and smoky white-tea notes add delicate flair to the finish.
title: Rooster Hill 2010 Medium Sweet Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: The 2010 Beaucastel is a tour de force, brilliantly combining espresso and black olive notes with bright raspberry fruit, while dark earthy notes provide a solid base. The feel on the palate is ample, with tannins that are a bit dusty but not tough or chewy. The long, mouthwatering finish bodes well for the future. Drink now–2030.
title: Famille Perrin 2010 Château de Beaucastel Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: This delivers a solid mix of red fruits, herbs and spices on the nose. The palate feels a little jagged, but it's forward and contains lively flavors of cherry, plum, nutmeg and brown sugar. It's fruity on the finish, with a dash of cocoa.
title: Casarena 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendoza)

description: Pours atypically dark for a Pinot Noir, and the color is reflected in the mouth, where the wine turns as heavy and full-bodied as Grenache. It's a good wine, hearty and flavorful in berries and cola, but lacks Pinot Noir's signature delicacy.
title: McIntyre Vineyards 2010 Estate Block 3 Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: This is the epitome of a composed, approachable Rioja reserva; it's earthy and dry up front, with aromas of fine herbs, brandied cherry and plum. A solid palate houses oaky flavors of vanilla and butter in addition to plum and currant, while the finish is dry, elegant and oaky enough to give off a popcorn-like character. Drink through 2022.
title: Bodegas Bilbainas 2010 Viña Pomal Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Not much Rutherford character here, in the way of structural complexity. What you get is a soft, gentle wine, with rich, ripe raspberry jam, cinnamon and toast flavors that are easy to like, especially with a juicy burger.
title: There 2010 Merlot (Rutherford)

description: About a second after you sip this wine, the sugary fruit hits, like the blast of a gummy bears. The wine is front-loaded with raspberries, watermelons, persimmons and strawberries, and the acidity is pretty good, but it's simply too sweet and fruity.
title: Balverne 2010 Estate Grown Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: This is a wine that feels heavy, with rich apricot flavors that lack freshness and balance. It's not a wine for aging, but will go well with cakes or pastries.
title: Rosenhof 2010 Orion Eiswein Grüner Veltliner (Burgenland)

description: The 2009 vintage of this wine was one of the stars from Georges Duboeuf, and this 2010 fits the same pattern. It has great richness, spice, black cherry fruits, the acidity offering the fruity side of the wine. Ready for aging two years.
title: Georges Duboeuf 2010 Domaine de la Chaponne  (Morgon)

description: This is a rich, fruity Chardonnay that's sweet in pineapple, orange and buttered toast flavors. A nice cut of acidity provides needed balance.
title: Clos La Chance 2010 Chardonnay (Monterey County)

description: New French oak dominates this wine with buttered toast and caramel flavors. Despite this heavy hand, the underlying wine somehow shines through, with orange, mango, pear and peach flavors, brightened with brisk acidity.
title: Lobo 2010 Wulff Vineyards Chardonnay (Oak Knoll District)

description: There's a slightly sour smell to this offering from Coomber Family, who produce “wines for animal lovers.” However, the sipping experience is pleasant enough, with hard cherry candy, a range of spices and a lingering licorice finish.
title: Coomber Family Ranch 2010 Private Reserve Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: A rustic, common wine; it's not quite ripe, and is hot in alcohol and pepper. But it's okay for everyday pizza and such.
title: Grayson 2010 Lot 12 Zinfandel (California)

description: Although this is dry and nicely tart in acidity, it's unripe and green, with a severe feline aroma to the citrus fruits.
title: Hill Wine Company 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (Napa Valley)

description: From a very cool vintage, this wine takes full advantage of the flavors the year delivered. Bracing and fresh, with a lively mix of lime and citrus fruits, its vivid acidity and a wash of wet rock, give it life and length across the palate. Complex and textural, this is a lovely wine.
title: L'Ecole No. 41 2010 Chardonnay (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Aromas suggest tilled soil, mature blackberry, leather, game and allspice. The lively palate offers prune, cherries marinated in spirits, ground black pepper and licorice alongside a firmly tannic backbone and brisk acidity. Drink 2016–2012.
title: Fongoli 2010 Riserva  (Montefalco Rosso)

description: Alluring aromas of violet, fresh herbs, mint and berry fruits are snappy and work well together. This smacks down hard in the mouth courtesy of lively acidity and tannic bite, while flavors of blackberry, toast, green herbs and tobacco finish narrow but firm.
title: Caliterra 2010 Tributo Single Vineyard Block # Espino Malbec (Colchagua Valley)

description: Herbal and short, this offers a very light watermelon flavor, along with plenty of herb and tomato leaf notes. It's on the slender side, with a quick-to-fade finish.
title: Erath 2010 Knight's Gambit Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: This single-vineyard offering opens with aromas of very mature—nearly overripe—black fruit and tilled soil. The palate is already evolved, showing fig, prune and cooked fruit along with espresso and oak. It finishes on a note of astringent wood tannins.
title: Livio Voghera 2010 Vigna Basarin  (Barbera d'Alba)

description: A blend of 80% Syrah and 20% Grenache, this boasts assertive aromas and flavors of ripe black cherry, raspberry, bramble and licorice root, with accents of black pepper and cocoa powder. The velvety tannins frame the dark fruit core on the palate, with lingering notes of dried fig and charred pepper on the close.
title: Château Tourril 2010 Panatella Syrah-Grenache (Minervois)

description: This opens with oak-driven aromas of toast, espresso, coffee and coconut. The concentrated palate offers oak, dried blackberry, ripe plum, roasted coffee bean, vanilla and a confectionary note alongside packed tannins. The fruit is starting to dry out so drink soon.
title: Castello Sonnino 2010 Castello di Montespertoli Riserva  (Chianti Montespertoli)

description: New wood aromas and flavors make this selection seem dominated by toast, giving it a dry, tannic character. The ripe plum fruit is submerged and is likely to surface only after 3–4 years of aging when a powerful, upfront wine will emerge.
title: Château l'Argenteyre 2010  Médoc

description: In a touch of irony, Versace is a no-frills Nero d'Avola that opens with a bright berry note and light touches of spice and toasted nut. The smooth finish would pair well with breaded veal.
title: Feudi del Pisciotto 2010 Versace Nero d'Avola (Sicilia)

description: Dry and soft in tannins, this exudes rich blackberry, grilled prosciutto and black pepper flavors. Made for drinking now, it shows real Santa Lucia  elegance at an affordable price.
title: Morgan 2010 Syrah (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Judging by the high-heeled shoe on the label, this is one of those wines aimed at the female consumer. It's Pinot in name only, with clean, simple cherry candy and oak tastes.
title: Haute Red 2010 Pinot Noir (California)

description: This Syrah is heavy and minty, with plastic aromas. The palate is chunky and citrusy in feel, with little body. Flavors of clove, mint and berry lead to a resiny, almost fruitless finish.
title: Botalcura 2010 Litú Syrah (Central Valley)

description: Darkly charred wood mingles with scents of coconut, dill and sweet spice on this heavily-oaked Chardonnay. Hints of smoke and hazelnuts seep into the palate as well, but serve to highlight fresh, fruity flavors of pear, lemon and orange rind. This finishes on a slightly-bitter wood note.
title: Truro 2010 Barrel Fermented Chardonnay (America)

description: This Cab is dry and properly tannic, with good blackberry, mocha and herb flavors. On the downside is a strange ashiness, like the grapes were hit by smoke from a wildfire. Drink up.
title: Mossback 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Chalk Hill)

description: Enormously ripe, forward and succulent in blackberries, cassis, raisins and chocolate, with rich tannins and a long, dry, spicy finish. Very fine, yet unresolved now, with internal conflicts. Wants some age, so give it 5–6 years, because high alcohol and overripeness will eventually dominate.
title: Chimney Rock 2010 Clone 7 Cabernet Sauvignon (Stags Leap District)

description: Hints of crushed slate lend a smoky, flinty character to concentrated flavors of tangerine and quince preserve in this beautiful off-dry Riesling. It's intensely fruity, but anchored in minerality from start to finish.
title: Ansgar Clüsserath 2010 Trittenheimer Apotheke Kabinett Riesling (Mosel)

description: A sculpted, refined expresion of Cabernet Sauvignon, Oatley's 2010 combines hints of mint and dark berries into a wine that offers clean, pure fruit, subtle oak shadings and a long, silky finish. Drink now–2020.
title: Robert Oatley 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Margaret River)

description: The fog reached a little too far into the vineyard in 2010, judging from this less-than-fully-ripe Pinot Noir. Although it has some decent cherry fruit, it lacks fruity richness, which makes the acidity stick out.
title: J. Lohr 2010 Fog's Reach Pinot Noir (Arroyo Seco)

description:
Brisk and clean, this dry white is the 
anti-Chardonnay, marked by tart citrus flavors that make it versatile with a wide range of foods.
title: Montevina 2010 Pinot Grigio (California)

description: This has a light herbaceous character followed by notes of warm yellow fruit, kiwi and bright acidity. It's crisp, fresh and ready to drink.
title: Château de Seguin 2010 Sauvignon de Seguin  (Bordeaux Blanc)

description: Dark in color, this delivers black fruit aromas along with a toasty note. While it's slightly hollow in the middle, it's wide around the edges, tasting of mossy blackberry and herb. The finish is toasty, big and grabby.
title: Viña La Fortuna 2010 Culpeo Made with Organic Grapes Merlot (Curicó Valley)

description: This hearty red opens with aromas of sunbaked earth, leather, menthol and aromatic herb. The solid, one-dimensional palate offers dried black cherry, blackberry confiture, licorice and tobacco alongside bracing, close-grained tannins that leave an astringent finish.
title: Tenuta Vitanza 2010 Andretta Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: The castle of the Hardegg family is a magnificent edifice near the Czech border with Austria. The wine from the castle vineyards is concentrated and rich, hinting at vanilla with intense ripe mango and peach flavors. There is pepper, acidity and ripe fruit. Screwcap.
title: Graf Hardegg 2010 Vom Schloss Grüner Veltliner (Niederösterreich)

description: Fermented and aged in neutral oak, this mixes the richness of Chardonnay with the herbaceous tang of Sauvignon Blanc—but flavors notwithstanding, it's in fact pure Viognier. Excellent mouthfeel and structure carry the light and pretty fruit onto the finish that shows just a hint of barrel toast.
title: Lullaby 2010 Viognier (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: This is a potent brew with complexities galore, but there's also a simplicity about it that's appealing. Its raw size is bracing, but the structure and fabric of this Malbec-Cabernet Sauvignon scream balance. Aromas of lead pencil, sawdust, coconut and brandied cherries introduce briary dark-berry flavors. A long, minty, power-packed finish says lay this down for a few years; drink from 2014–2020.
title: Pascual Toso 2010 Magdalena Toso Limited 20 Barrels Malbec-Cabernet Sauvignon (Mendoza)

description: Dark and earthy, with a streak of soy and a hint of funk, this complex effort opens powerfully, then turns more subtle, with plum and chocolate highlights. It's one of those wines that never stops evolving in the glass.
title: K Vintners 2010 Cougar Hills Syrah (Walla Walla Valley (WA))

description: A blend of Riesling, Pinot Gris, Gewurztraminer and Muscat, this is a wine that fascinates. Both fresh and rich, this wine combines strongly perfumed notes with a pure, steely texture. It is drinkable now, although it will be better in three years' time. Glass stopper
title: Domaine Pfister 2010 Cuvée 8 White (Alsace)

description: Made in a leaner, racier style than most California Viogniers, this has mineral, tangerine and apricot flavors, with considerable acidity.
title: Ojai 2010 Roll Ranch Vineyard Viognier (California)

description: Soothing menthol and green floral notes lend vibe to crisp sour-cherry flavors in this bold, penetrating Bordeaux-style blend. It's rich and brawny on the palate with juicy, primary cherry and plum flavors, yet streaked with delicate touches of oak and earth.
title: Anthony Nappa 2010 Dieci Red (North Fork of Long Island)

description: A very structured, firm wine with excellent acidity and dark, dry tannins. The alcohol does show through, but the wine is firm and very dense, a big success from this Lurton family owned property.
title: Château Bouscaut 2010  Pessac-Léognan

description: A villages-style Pinot, this has a mouthfeel that scours, with flavors of sour cherry candy, cola and sandalwood flavors.
title: La Crema 2010 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Rich flavors of orange and tropical fruit are marked with brisk acidity and a touch of Champagne-like sourdough yeast. The finish is sugary and simple.
title: Rail2Rail 2010 Sur Lie Chardonnay (Monterey)

description: Snappy, crisp and green on the nose, but well balanced and showing the good side of Chilean SB, which is oceanic, fresh, green and citrusy. Flavors of lime, grapefruit and passion fruit are true, while the finish is fruity at first before breaking up.
title: Errazuriz 2010 Estate Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: Celebrating 500 years as grape growers, the Mellot family offers this top Sancerre. It is rounded and spicy with a touch of wood to add to its rich character. Apricots and pineapple are a delicious addition to the citrus acidity that cuts right through the wine. Age for 3–4 years.
title: Joseph Mellot 2010 La Grande Châtelaine  (Sancerre)

description: A bright red wine rustic in black plum and cherry flavor, lightweight and easy, a rightful companion to spaghetti and meatballs or even a BLT.
title: Auriga 2010 Smokey Ridge Ranch Sangiovese (El Dorado)

description: Holly's Hill Mourvèdre opens with a gigantic waft of black pepper, followed by intense fruit flavors of cherry and dried plum. Medium bodied and light on the finish, this earthy wine also gives off a hint of cured meats, with which it would pair nicely.
title: Holly's Hill 2010 Estate Mourvèdre (El Dorado)

description: Bright, jammy aromas and ripe, lively, chocolate and cherry flavors give a lot of personality to this velvet-textured wine. Medium to full bodied, it has a good blanket of fine tannins to coat the tongue and give the wine grip.
title: Ironstone 2010 Reserve Malbec (Lodi)

description: Open this wine and you'll feel as though you've stepped into an Italian deli—so finely has it captured the smell of cured meats. The back layers feature cherry and herb, the finish a burst of roasted, toasted oak. This is a great wine to pair with a range of Italian-inspired meals.
title: Sorelle Winery 2010 Troppo Bella Sangiovese (Lodi)

description: 100% varietal with all of the fruit coming from StoneTree Vineyard, it's barrel-forward, with notes of fresh brewed coffee, dark chocolate and cherry. It starts out lighter in style before fleshing out over time with a pleasing interplay between the oak and fruit flavors. Soft tannins and tart acidity provide the frame.
title: Knight Hill 2010 StoneTree Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Wahluke Slope)

description: This is a finely balanced, ripe wine. It is medium sweet and ripe with fruit, but counterpointed with taut structure and acidity. It's ready to drink, but will be better with some aging. Drink until 2020.
title: Domaine Albert Mann 2010 Furstentum Grand Cru Vieilles Vignes Gewurztraminer (Alsace)

description: Spice, herb, smoke and pencil-eraser aromas lead to elegant, tart fruit flavors that don't seem to have fully ripened.
title: Almquist 2010 Clifton Vineyard Zinfandel (Wahluke Slope)

description: This has loud, vivid aromas of hazelnuts and roasted popcorn kernels. It has a luscious medium to full-bodied palate, and it bears bold, protracted honeyed flavors. Halibut en croute should prove a rockin' pairing.
title: Michael Shaps 2010 Wild Meadow Vineyard Chardonnay (Virginia)

description: Gorgeous, amplified aromas of wild berry, plum and fine herbs are crystal clear and magnetic. This beautiful Priorat is layered, smooth as glass and exemplary in every way. Flavors of mixed black fruits, cocoa, toast and schisty mineral finish in a swirl of chocolate, blackened toast, licorice and peppercorn. Best from 2015–2018.
title: Clos Mogador 2010 Red (Priorat)

description: This Catalonian blend is a concentrated number with a narrow, fruit-forward nose and cheesy leather on the side. The palate is full, grabby and ripe; flavors of blackberry and cassis are jammy and dark. It finishes sweet in flavor and ultraripe in feel, with a syrupy aftertaste. Another year or two in bottle will help this settle down; drink through 2020.
title: Vinyes del Terrer 2010 Terrer d'Aubert Red (Tarragona)

description: This blend of premier crus and some village Côte de Beaune shows generous red-cherry fruit and a touch of acidity. Its depth of richness and complexity highlight its fine origins.
title: Joseph Drouhin 2010  Côte de Beaune

description: A rounded, wood-aged wine, this is full of ripe red-berry and spice flavors, with a core of warm, smoky tannins. It is structured and should age over several years.
designation: Domaine de la Créa Les Grands Picotins
title: Louis Max 2010 Domaine de la Créa Les Grands Picotins  (Savigny-lès-Beaune)

description: Dense aromas of berries come with graphite and peppery scents, with notes of wood smoke and grilled meat. This feels full and a touch rough and rugged, with raspberry and plum flavors matched by herbal and burnt accents. It's forward and toasted on the finish, with coffee, herb and animal notes. Contains 15% Cabernet Sauvignon.
title: Finca las Nubes 2010 Malbec (Cafayate)

description: Dark plum and berry aromas come with a touch of rubber. It feels bright, healthy and juicy, with good body and earthy berry and dark plum flavors. While it's not complicated, it's accurate and flavorful.
title: Vega Sindoa 2010 Tempranillo (Navarra)

description: Gold color, this is a delicious ripe wine from the small section of the Layon valley that also has the Quarts de Chaume vineyard. The honeyed character is cut through with dry botrytis, baked apples and brown sugar, balanced by intense acidity.
title: Château de la Roulerie 2010 Les Aunis Chenin Blanc (Coteaux du Layon Chaume)

description: Shows the hallmarks of a fine Mount Veeder Cabernet, namely immediate succulence, intensely concentrated mountain fruit, and yet an almost certain capacity to age. Lush, ripe flavors of blackberries and crème de cassis lead to a long, soft finish. Drink now–2018.
title: Crosby Roamann 2010 Cupiens Videre Lucem Cabernet Sauvignon (Mount Veeder)

description: This wine is atypically fine and chiseled for this estate, combining bright, sweet fruity notes of lime and green apples with crushed stone and spring flowers. It's light in body, but impeccably balanced.
title: Dr. Pauly Bergweiler 2010 Bernkasteler Badstube Spätlese Riesling (Mosel)

description: This bright Aglianico delivers a dark fruit aroma, with touches of rum cake and dusty mineral at the back. The brightness of the acidity will refresh your tastebuds.
title: Terredora 2010 Aglianico (Campania)

description: This 100% varietal wine hails from Canyon Vineyard Ranch and Oasis vineyards. It's tart and puckering with notes of cocoa, raspberries and licorice and a firm squeeze of tannins. Barrel notes of vanilla and dill linger on the finish.
title: Waterbrook 2010 Merlot (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Bulky, stewy, jammy aromas of prune and raisin are an accurate indication of what's to come. This is pure prune juice, with mushy weight and sweet, syrupy flavors. Fig, raisin and chocolate flavors pop up on the finish, but overall this is soupy and drastically overripe.
title: Clos Pons 2010 810 Red (Costers del Segre)

description: Smoke wafts amidst savory notes of apple on this dry, slightly bitter Riesling. Crisp lemony acidity lends refreshment on the palate.
title: Fulkerson 2010 Reserve Riesling (Finger Lakes)

description: Just what a Dry Creek Zin should be. It's dry, furry in tannins, heady in alcohol and with enough acidity to give brightness to the wild berry, currant, dark chocolate, tobacco and spice flavors. Classic in its way, and nice to drink now.
title: J. Rickards 2010 Voigt Family Vineyards Zinfandel (Dry Creek Valley)

description: Paul Dolan Zin remains big with savory flavors beef jerky, roast meat and black pepper. Not overdone in alcohol or tannin, its a perfect go-to for nights around the grill and will please a crowd.
title: Paul Dolan 2010 Zinfandel (Mendocino County)

description: Bell peppers and green herbs as well as dark tannins are the main characters of this very dry, bitter wine.
title: Château des Tourtes 2010  Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Juicy and fruity, this has apple and lemon flavors that combine with a steely edge. It's refreshing and crisp, with a bright, lively character, and a tang on the finish.
title: La Chablisienne 2010 La Sereine  (Chablis)

description: Of all the excellent Genium 2010s, this is the only one that's a bit stalky on the nose. It's also rubbery smelling, with tight-grained oak scents and earth notes. Hard, tannic and without much cushion, this Garnacha-led blend tastes earthy, stalky and of high-toned plum and currant with lemony oak. A bold, tannic finish says this will last through about 2024.
title: Genium Celler 2010 Poboleda Vi de la Vila Red (Priorat)

description: Made from young vines, this is the second wine of Suduiraut. It has young, honeyed character, with a ripe, opulent texture that lays bitter orange fruits over drier noble rot flavors. It is so delicious now, but will be better from 2017.
title: Château Suduiraut 2010 Castelnau de Suduiraut  (Sauternes)

description: Pungent tobacco, sage and grape juice aromas lead the nose on this tasty wine. The tannins are well dispersed on the palate, and the flavors balance between dark purple fruits and herbals of licorice and crushed marjoram.
title: Blackjack 2010 Billy Goat Hill Merlot (Santa Barbara County)

description: Aromas suggesting toast, hazelnut, camphor and mocha lead the nose. The full-bodied palate delivers ripe wild cherry, white pepper, grilled sage and anise alongside close-grained tannins that leave an assertive finish.
title: Cascina Ballarin 2010 Bricco Rocca Tistot Riserva  (Barolo)

description: A perfectly fine Cabernet, with ripe blackberry, black currant and herb flavors. Does a good job at a fair price.
title: Michael Pozzan 2010 Annabella Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa County)

description: An attractive, crisp wine that boasts all the right citrus and green herb flavors along with a hint of pepper. Bright and ready to drink. Screwcap
title: Pratsch 2010 Sandgrube Grüner Veltliner (Weinviertel)

description: Structured yet elegant, this blend of Sangiovese, Sagrantino and a drop of Cabernet Sauvignon delivers a medley of sensations including crushed blue flower, black cherry, blackberry, ground white pepper, savory herb, graphite and cinnamon-spice. It's impeccably balanced with fresh acidity and firm but refined tannins. Drink through 2020.
title: Adanti 2010  Montefalco Rosso

description: Juicy and floral with dusty tannins, this Lodi blend of 75% Cabernet Sauvignon and 25% Sangiovese exhibits the best of both worlds, with a well-integrated taste of soft raspberry jam, cranberry and earth.
title: Sorelle Winery 2010 Russo Red Cabernet Sauvignon-Sangiovese (Lodi)

description: This is an exuberant, happy wine, filled with sunny summer flavors of cherries, blueberries and blackberries. The blend is based on Zinfandel and Grenache. It's rich and soft in tannins and easy to drink. The alcohol is a little high, but nothing that barbecue can't handle.
title: Proulx 2010 Dimples Red (Paso Robles)

description: A 100% varietal wine, this is both soft and dense, herbal and restrained in cranberry and red currant. Dry and dusty, the body is medium to full bodied, and the finish medium-long. Enjoy now.
title: Arbios 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)

description: Salty is not a common descriptor for Pinot Gris, but appropriate here. Crisp apple, Asian pear, unripe peach, a dusting of cinnamon, and some slightly bitter skin flavors combine here; the acids rule.
title: Foris 2010 Pinot Gris (Rogue Valley)

description: American oak is the barrel choice, 100% new, and it shows strongly in the nose of this young Zinfandel. The citrusy acids help to balance out the alcohol, and with additional bottle age the score could certainly rise. If you plan to drink this soon, give it plenty of breathing time.
title: Thurston Wolfe 2010 Reserve Zinfandel (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: The Red Queen bottling is Evening Land's most limited in terms of production. The grapes are sourced from a block in the estate vineyard at the highest point of the hill, and the wine is aged in 30% new French oak for 14 months. Tasted a few weeks prior to its official release, it was stubbornly unyielding, even after a full 24 hours. Given the pedigree of this estate, and the superb quality of the two other 2010 Pinots, it is reasonable to expect this to come out of its hard shell and earn a much higher score.
title: Evening Land 2010 Seven Springs Vineyard Red Queen Pinot Noir (Eola-Amity Hills)

description: Musky, spicy and reasonably complex on the nose, this recent entrant into the ranks of realistically priced Riesling is a bit chunky in the mouth, but offers satisfying apple, melon and citrus flavors.
title: Redbreast 2010 Riesling (Mosel)

description: Honig's basic Napa Cab is a pleasant wine to drink now. It shows an elegance of tannins and acids, with well-ripened blackberry, black currant and cedar flavors that finish with refinement.
title: Honig 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Coming from a warm site in a cool vintage, it's an intriguing wine with notes of freshly cut hay, peach and mango.  It's lighter in style, with the partial malolactic fermentation (60%) providing extra weight and feel.
title: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2010 Canoe Ridge Estate Chardonnay (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: Aromas of dark cherry, mocha and grilled game lead to the full-bodied palate, which has firm, muscular tannins and bright acids. Subtle notes of wild berry, undergrowth and tobacco slowly surface, lending this Syrah extra appeal.
title: Page Springs 2010 Clone 474 Colibri Vineyard Syrah (Cochise County)

description: ). You'll want to cellar this young 100% Cab because it's not showing its full potential yet. Dry and tannic, it's very rich in black currants and blackberry jam, but all of the wine's parts need more time to fully integrate; currently, the oak is forward, the acids are tart and citrusy, and the tannins are fierce. Should improve over the next 10 years, maybe even 20.
title: Venge 2010 Family Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Working with both Klipsun and Ciel du Cheval, winemaker Casey McClellan extracts the essence of both these great vineyards in his Red Mountain cuvées. Here is the raw power of 100% Klipsun Cabernet Sauvignon, full-throttle black fruits and mineral-soaked, chewy, dark tannins. It's all done in a wine with both length and precision; one that will surely age well over at least a decade.
title: Seven Hills 2010 Klipsun Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Red Mountain)

description: This Syrah shows ripe blackberry, mulberry and currant flavors, plus notes of smoked meat, charred bone, anise and black pepper. With firm tannins and fine minerality, this clonal Syrah has great structure. It's terrific to drink now after some time in a decanter, and it can also age for 6–8 more years.
title: Brander 2010 Colson Canyon Vineyard Clone 877 Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

description: Bold, ripe fruit and chocolate notes open the nose of this blend of Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot. The wine is layered and rich, with lingering tones of tobacco and sweet spice.
title: Casa di Terra 2010 Mosaico  (Bolgheri)

description: Scents of cedar and forest floor linger prominently throughout this delicately fruity Cabernet Franc. Tart cranberry and sour cherry flavors keep the round palate crisp and elegant.
title: Chateau Lafayette Reneau 2010 Cabernet Franc (Finger Lakes)

description: From an excellent vintage, this bright Rosso offers creamy aromas of espresso mousse and milk chocolate, with steady pulses of cherry and blackberry at the back. The wine's fresh acidity pairs well with cheesy pasta or pizza.
title: Castiglion del Bosco 2010  Rosso di Montalcino

description: Clean, pure and uncomplicated on the nose, showing depth and clarity. Smells and tastes much like a good Malbec or Cabernet might; it's sweet and fruity, with black cherry, pastry, baking spices and a certain rubbery quality that seems to come with Bonarda. On the money for this variety.
title: Maipe 2010 Reserve Old Vine Bonarda (Mendoza)

description: A screwcapped, Rhône-style white, blends 43% Viognier, 39% Roussanne and 18% Marsanne in a tightly wound, fresh, lively wine. Spicy apple and pear fruit flavors run into a medium long finish with some near-term aging potential.
title: Airfield Estates 2010 Lightning White White (Yakima Valley)

description: Molamatta is a blend of Pinot Bianco, Friulano and Ribolla Gialla that opens with a pretty golden color and rich aromas of creamy peach and vanilla mousse with pretty highlights of citrus and light spice. This feels rich and long on the palate.
title: Marco Felluga 2010 Molamatta White (Collio)

description: If you're looking for a decent everyday Chardonnay at an inexpensive price, consider this one. It's dry and creamy in texture, with slightly sweet, oaky tangerine and pineapple flavors.
title: CK Mondavi 2010 Willow Springs Chardonnay (California)

description: Sweet and herbal smelling, then firm and tight in the mouth, with clampy tannins and generic, dark flavors of black plum and berry. Feels a little reduced and heavy on the finish.
title: Cousiño-Macul 2010 Merlot (Maipo Valley)

description: This wine shows the richness of the vineyard, offering flashy flavors of raspberry and cherry jam, licorice, brown sugar and  smoky oak. Balanced with bright acidity, this is meant to be consumed now and over the next few years.
title: Lucienne 2010 Lone Oak Vineyard Pinot Noir (Santa Lucia Highlands)

description: Jammy, dark aromas form a good opening, and there's blueberry and rubbery notes as well. The mouthfeel is entirely full and chunky, with grabby tannins in support of the spicy core flavors of licorice, blackberry, pepper and espresso. Oaky, toasty and a touch hot on the finish.
title: Echeverria 2010 Reserva Syrah (Colchagua Valley)

description: Dry and crisp in acidity, with a silky texture, this is a polished Pinot Noir for drinking now. It has a rich array of cherry pie and sweet oak flavors, with earthier notes of pomegranate and cola.
title: Arista 2010 Two Birds Estate Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: The Dead Arm is always firmly structured, making it one of the more challenging Shiraz to judge in its youth. The 2010 is full-bodied and chewy, characterized by dark, earthy and umami notes of roasted meat, asphalt and grilled mushrooms. The length and ripely tannic nature of the finish is reassuring. Drink 2018–2030.
title: D'Arenberg 2010 The Dead Arm Shiraz (McLaren Vale)

description: There are subtle tones of brimstone, pear, apricot and citrus on this wine. You can feel the alcohol here, although it is pretty well integrated within the wine's crisp, smooth mouthfeel.
title: Rocca del Principe 2010  Fiano di Avellino

description: Plum, berry and milk chocolate aromas are not too aggressive. The palate is round and friendly feeling, with flavors of wild berry, sweet oak and carob. Finishes with solid oaky flavors. Good Syrah that's uncomplicated and generally well made.
title: Siegel 2010 Crucero Syrah (Colchagua Valley)

description: This has ripe blackberry and oak flavors. It's dry, full bodied and tannic enough to cut through a rich, ketchup-strewn cheeseburger.
title: Lost Angel 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)

description: If you like tart, less-than-ripe Sauvignon Blanc, you'll enjoy this one. It's acidic, but relieved by lemon, lime and honey flavors.
title: White Oak 2010 Sauvignon Blanc (Russian River Valley)

description: In the past, the winery made a Mendocino appellation Chardonnay from a mix of Anderson Valley and Potter Valley fruit. But in 2010, all of the fruit is sourced from the Anderson Valley. This is fleshy, soft and lush, bearing grapefruit and fig flavors that are wrapped in a tightly focused package with a bright finish.
title: Navarro 2010 Chardonnay (Mendocino)

description: This boasts oak-driven aromas of honey and vanilla, with notes of exotic fruit, citrus and Golden Delicious. It is soft and supple, with a lively, fruit-driven finish.
title: Marisa Cuomo 2010 Furore Bianco Fiorduva White (Costa d'Amalfi)

description: Apple and grass aromas are clean and basic. For a brut nature, this has a plump feel and fruity roundness. Yeasty flavors of baked white bread and briny apple and nectarine finish mellow and smooth. Drink now.
designation: Brut Nature Gran Reserva Reserva de la Familia
title: Juvé y Camps 2010 Brut Nature Gran Reserva Reserva de la Familia Sparkling (Cava)

description: A little rough and oily on the nose, with citrus and green notes. The palate is soft and melony in feel, with banana, papaya and other tropical flavors. Sweet and melony on the finish; won't last for very long.
title: Santa Carolina 2010 Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Leyda Valley)

description: From an Umpqua Valley vineyard, this solid and fruit-driven Pinot Gris needs a little more bottle age to come together. The pear fruit flavor is there in abundance, but some off-notes creep in, damping the finish.
title: Seven Hills 2010 Pinot Gris (Oregon)

description: Raw oak, herb and floral aromas are complex and on the light side of the fence. This feels tight, tannic and edgy, while resiny oak gives off a lactic flavor to accompany core red plum and currant. This stays tight, punchy and drying on the finish. Drink through 2021.
title: Bodegas Berceo 2010 Viña Berceo Crianza  (Rioja)

description: A great find, this is fully ripe but not too full bodied, offering opulent dark plum and black currant aromas, broad and rich flavors, and a plush, velvety texture. Subtle cedar and spice tones from oak barrels add complexity to the dark fruit notes, and soft tannins fill in the texture and help the finish linger.
title: Sol Rouge 2010 Cabernet Franc (Red Hills Lake County)

description: D'Arenberg wines are typically quite structured, so this wine's full body and creamy texture come as a bit of a surprise. Earthy, loamy notes run alongside espresso, black olive and blueberry before finishing long and silky. Drink now–2020.
title: D'Arenberg 2010 Shipster's Rapture Single Vineyard Shiraz (McLaren Vale)

description: It's hard not to be a big fan of Giacomo Vico's beautiful Chardonnay. The wine marries the cool-climate characteristics of the Langhe (Piedmont) with a round, opulent and rich winemaking style. You'll recognize loads of yellow fruit and rose with a playful touch of sweet vanilla.
title: Giacomo Vico 2010 Chardonnay (Langhe)

description: Textbook Sonoma Petite Sirah, midnight black in color, high in alcohol and enormously thick in blackberry, cassis and chocolate, with a bacony, funky note of cured meat. It's delicious now with richly-flavored meats and cheeses.
title: Mounts 2010 Petasera Estate Grown Petite Sirah (Dry Creek Valley)

description: A burly Primitivo with spicy blackberry fruit, this is an intense wine with layers of smoke and oak to offer those who like a wine that not only pairs with a slab of meat but almost tastes like one.
title: Sobon Estate 2010 ReZerve Primitivo (Amador County)

description: Chardonnay (75%) and Grillo are blended to produce an intense wine with pungent notes of ripe fruit, pineapple, candied lemon and yellow rose. There is alcoholic heat on the close but it is softened by the general density and thickness of the wine's texture.
title: Baglio del Cristo di Campobello 2010 Adènzia White (Sicilia)

description: Terre di Giumara is a fresh and tight white wine with bright yellow fruit, peach and honey-roasted almond. The wine is full and ripe, with a touch of piquant spice on the close.
title: Caruso & Minini 2010 Terre di Giumara Inzolia (Sicilia)

description: Light and juicy, this has fresh blackcurrant fruit, with balanced tannins. With its fruit forward flavor, it's not a wine for aging.
title: Rigal 2010 The Original Malbec (Vin de Pays du Lot)

description: This crisp Chardonnay has a disjointedness about it. Its aroma and flavor is most akin to Lemonheads candy.
title: Matchbook 2010 Old Head Chardonnay (Dunnigan Hills)

description: This wine is 42% Cabernet Sauvignon, 26% Merlot, 23% Cabernet Franc and 9% Malbec from Hedges Estate, Red Mountain, Stillwater and Weinbau vineyards. It's youthful at present, with aromas of woodspice, coffee, blackberry and graphite. The palate displays muscular fruit flavors surrounded by a framework of dry, chewy tannins.
title: Tenor 2010 1:1 Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Bramble and savory herbs add a green, earthy character to preserved black cherry and plum flavors in this dry, full-bodied Merlot. The tannins are penetrating, but velvety in structure, and extend long on the cherry-kissed finish.
title: Keuka Spring 2010 Merlot (Finger Lakes)

description: A rich, chocolaty wine, with the structure right up front. Under the same ownership as Château Montrose, this is a wine that reveals all its attractions early with sweet blackberry fruit and powerful tannins. There is some freshness as well, giving fruitiness and acidity at the end.
title: Château Tronquoy-Lalande 2010  Saint-Estèphe

description: Hints of mint, cedar and blue flower add depth to the black cherry aromas on this blend of 45% Montepulciano, 45% Sangiovese and 10% Cabernet Sauvignon. Juicy fruit flavors carry over to fresh and accessible palate. Not for cellaring; enjoy now.
title: Le Caniette 2010 Rosso Bello  (Rosso Piceno)

description: This is a sound but heavy wine. Superripe raspberry and cherry jam flavors feels syrupy and unbalanced on the palate, partly due to it's high alcohol level.
title: Marshall Family Wines 2010 Fox in the Hen House Pinot Noir (Carneros)

description: A fresh and fruity wine, with black currant notes, yet somewhat stalky in character. The aftertaste is bright and light.
title: Château Anniche 2010  Côtes de Bordeaux

description: Burnt, stalky, toasted aromas dominate this inexpensive Garnacha, and there's not much freshness to the nose as a result. In the mouth, it's more of the same; things are heavy, with fully oaked flavors of medicinal berry, hickory and barbecued meat.
title: Lo Nuevo 2010 Sorbo a Sorbo Old Vines Garnacha (Calatayud)

description: For the price, this is one of the best Cab values in the market. It shows fine varietal character, with blackberry and cherry flavors wrapped into firm tannins and a spicy finish.
title: Nickname 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)

description: Another fine Cabernet from Prime, following a great 2009. This '10 is perhaps a little off pace from the prior vintage, but is nonetheless impressive. Dry and smoothly tannic, it shows complicated blackberry, blueberry, black currant, sage and mineral flavors. Give it at least six years in a good cellar.
title: Prime 2010 Cabernet Sauvignon (Coombsville)

description: Since this is 100% Sauvignon Blanc, it is intensely crisp, with grapefruit flavors and bright acidity that lead into a lemongrass aftertaste. It's clean and fresh, and is likely to mature quickly.
title: Château Couhins-Lurton 2010  Pessac-Léognan

description: Firm, dark and finely tannic, this has weight and richness, with a severity that dominates the fruitiness. It's dry and tough, and it's aging potential is questionable.
title: Château Castera 2010  Médoc

description: This is a smoky, wood-aged wine, with a bitter toast flavor that smothers the fruit. It does have the fruit weight, so that toast flavor will certainly soften. But to judge by the intense wood character, this bottling will likely always have a severe edge.
title: Château Fleur la Mothe 2010  Médoc

description: Dry and tannic, this Pinot is heavy. It has strong flavors of blackstrap molasses, tea, black cherry jam and oak, and is ready to drink now.
title: Peters Family 2010 Pinot Noir (Sonoma Mountain)

description: This is heady for Chilean Chardonnay, with baked apple and no shortage of toast on the bouquet. It feels fresh but full, with polish and elegance to the mouthfeel. Flavors of apple and spiced squash end in a whirl of cinnamon and mild oak-related resin, while a final touch of acidity keeps things lively.
title: Concha y Toro 2010 Amelia Limited Release Chardonnay (Casablanca Valley)

description: There's a slightly sour, forest-floor funkiness to the nose of this wine from an historic appellation near Hollister. Not boasting much ripe fruit on the palate, the flavors lean toward leather and earth, with fine tannins, nice acidity and hints of licorice.
title: Pietra Santa 2010 Estate Merlot (Cienega Valley)

description: With firm, smoky tannins and a dark bitter-cherry flavor, this is a dense wine. It is fully ripe and rich, with a firm kick at the back. Tough and age worthy, it expresses the schist soil of the region.
title: Duorum 2010 Quinta do Castelo Melhor Vintage  (Port)

description: The former home of Alexis Lichine is now owned by a Pacific-based French mining company. The low yields of the 2010 (the vineyard was hit by hail) have produced a smooth and rich style of wine, which is weighty while also showing fine acidity on the finish. This has definite aging potential.
title: Château Prieuré-Lichine 2010  Margaux

description: Toast, oak, coconut and mocha aromas take center stage and carry over to the palate along with star anise and baking spice. It lacks fruit richness while bracing tannins grip the finish.
title: Cavalier Bartolomeo 2010 San Lorenzo  (Barolo)

description: Made with Sangiovese, this shows ripe berry and black pepper aromas with a whiff of tobacco leaf. The bright palate delivers a tight core of black cherry accented with mint, white pepper and a hint of cocoa. Pair this with barbecued meats and grilled vegetables.
title: Tenuta di Trecciano 2010 Terra Rossa Riserva  (Chianti Colli Senesi)

description: This medium-bodied wine is fresh and fruity, but also polished and sophisticated. It smells like raspberries and black plums, then adds chocolate and blackberry in the flavors. Rich and flavorful it also has welcome firm tannins and fruit acidity to keep it lively. Grenache is the leading grape ingredient.
title: Fenestra 2010 Estate Infrared Red (Livermore Valley)

description: This is a soft, silky-textured version of Crozes-Hermitage. Hints of cedar accent black cherry and licorice aromas, while the flavors are super ripe, verging on jammy. It's already approachable, and should drink well through 2020.
title: Duvernay 2010  Crozes-Hermitage

description: As the name suggests, this blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon does taste big and bold, but it also tastes hot and lopsided. It has a Port-like consistency, with notes of charred sesame seed, chocolate and dark cherry that lack precision.
title: Tarara 2010 Whitie's D9 A Big Bold Red (Virginia)

description: This wine is rubbery and gaseous on the bouquet, with compost, creamy oak and milk chocolate scents. The feel is full but jumbled with jammy black fruit flavors.
title: Lomas del Valle 2010 Coastal Cool Climate Syrah (Casablanca Valley)

description: There's a nice concentration of black-bramble fruit on the nose, with appealing notes of green peppercorn and anise. The moderately-full palate is tight, tannic and needs time in the glass to release the brooding black berry and plum flavors. A lift of menthol on the finish adds some levity.
title: DuCard 2010 Petit Verdot (Virginia)

description: Lean, tart and slightly ungenerous on the palate, but the flavors are authentically Pinot Noir, ranging from earth and root vegetables to pie cherries and herbs. Clean and crisp on the finish.
title: Cooralook 2010 Pinot Noir (Strathbogie Ranges)

description: Scratchy aromas of red-berry jam are floral and lightly perfumed. This is spongy and dense in feel, with good focus. Deep berry and chocolate flavors take on a molasses note in front of a chocolate and fig finish. Drink through 2018.
title: Bodegas San Isidro 2010 Gémina Cuvée Monastrell (Jumilla)

description: Dusty tannins push to the front, resulting in a firm, wood-aged wine that shows smokiness, ripe blackberry flavors and a firm core. This should be at its best in 2–3 years.
title: Château Maison Neuve 2010 Clotilde  (Blaye Côtes de Bordeaux)

description: Made using fruit sourced from 40-year-old vines, this elegant and detailed Pinot does not shy away from flavors of leaf and herb. But they are anchored in pure and expressive fruit, with old-vine flavors of bramble and pepper. What is most exceptional is the delicacy and length of this wine, two characteristics shared by all the Kelley Fox wines.
title: Kelley Fox 2010 Maresh Vineyard Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: This delicately effervescent sparkling blend of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay is fresh and fragrant like a just-bitten apple. Quite dry on the palate, it balances elegant berry and rosehip flavors with nuanced minerality and a shower of zippy acidity.
title: Wölffer 2010 Noblesse Oblige Extra Brut Rosé  (The Hamptons, Long Island)

description: Juicy aromas of blackberry and blueberry fruit pick up some baking spice notes on the nose of this bottling from atop the mountains above the Malibu Coast. The flavors are also very fruit-forward, with candied blueberry and clove spice.
title: Colcanyon 2010 Merlot

description: This dark, ripe and modern Syrah features wild berry, leather and cheesy smelling oak on the bouquet. It's plump and grabby, with plenty of oak and raw tannin to support black-fruit, vanilla and spice flavors. Oak dominates the finish, which has good density, weight and length.
region_1: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla y León
title: Clunia 2010 Syrah (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla y León)

description: A very soft wine, with its yellow fruits giving a rounded character. It is warm, forward and ready to drink.
title: Domaines Devillard 2010 Le Renard  (Bourgogne)

description: Aromas of Spanish broom, honeysuckle and a yeasty note lead the nose of this Sicilian sparkler from Mount Etna. The palate offers ripe apple, candied citrus and bitter walnuts alongside persistent bubbles and zesty acidity.
title: Murgo 2010 Brut Metodo Classico Nerello Mascalese (Vino Spumante)

description: Loamy, ripe aromas of raisin and prune are neither exact nor direct. This has a heavy, tannic mouthfeel with balance issues. Rubbery plum and prune flavors finish with a soft, spread-out feel.
title: Señorio de Valdiestro 2010 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: With a strong undercurrent of bell pepper, this soft Petite Sirah has tense shadings of peppery spice and leather. Ready to drink now.
title: Concannon 2010 Nina's Cuvée Petite Sirah (Livermore Valley)

description: This is a rich and concentrated wine, packed with tannins and dense, perfumed berry fruits. There is a fresh element to the wine also, that gives a lift to the aftertaste. For a lighter, softer style of LBV, this is just right.
title: C. da Silva 2010 Presdential  (Port)

description: As delicious as Pinot Noir gets, this has fireworks of raspberries, cherries and cola, and a sweet, mushroom-like essence of earth. Then the depth kicks in. It's not a big, tannic wine, but rather soft, with enormous charm and immediate appeal. Drink now–2016.
title: Acacia 2010 Roberts Road Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: A sweet-smelling wine, with notes of cranberry, white pepper, and Band Aid. It has tart fruit flavors and firm tannins.
title: Hood Crest 2010 Vintners Select Sangiovese Sangiovese (Columbia Gorge (OR))

description: Graphite, maple and other oaky aromas dominate this lush, pitch-black Tinto Fino. A chewy, dense mouthfeel is rich as an oil baron, while oak-driven flavors of vanilla and baking spices accent blackberry and cassis. On the finish, this is monster-sized and full of blue-fruit deliciousness. Drink through 2020.
title: Emilio Moro 2010 Malleolus de Sanchomartín  (Ribera del Duero)

description: At almost two-thirds Cabernet Sauvignon, this is a dark, intense, cassis-laden wine. It's full-bodied and richly textured, with obvious cellaring potential of at least 7 or 8 years. Dark chocolate and gentle coffee notes creep in on the midpalate, while the long, mouthwatering finish features layers of soft tannins.
title: Babich 2010 The Patriarch Premium Red Wine Red (Hawke's Bay)

description: The alcohol is hefty but balanced, giving the wine a heat it wears well. The tannins are big and thick, while the fruit is all about ripe black cherries and chocolate. It hits the sweet spot, but finishes dry. Drink now–2018.
title: Raymond 2010 District Collection Cabernet Sauvignon (St. Helena)
